TYPICAL PEDON.content:
Typical Pedon:
Altitude clay loam, 3 percent slope under cultivated grass in a park, at an elevation of 1,592 meters. (Colors are for dry soil unless otherwise noted.)
^A--0 to 14 centimeters; grayish brown (10YR 5/2) clay loam, very dark grayish brown (10YR 3/2) moist; moderate fine granular structure, slightly hard, firm, moderately sticky, moderately plastic; 10 percent subrounded fine and medium gravel; noneffervescent; neutral (pH 7.0); clear wavy boundary.
^C--14 to 70 centimeters; brown (7.5YR 5/4) very gravelly sandy loam, brown (7.5YR 4/4) moist; massive, slightly hard, friable, slightly sticky, slightly plastic; 50 percent subrounded fine and medium gravel; finely disseminated free carbonates in the soil matrix; slightly effervescent; slightly alkaline (pH 7.6); clear wavy boundary.
^Cu1--70 to 111 centimeters; brown (7.5YR 5/3) sandy loam, dark brown (7.5YR 3/3) moist; massive; slightly hard, friable, slightly sticky, slightly plastic; 11 percent subrounded fine and medium gravel; 5 percent artifacts of coal combustion; finely disseminated free carbonates in the soil matrix; strongly effervescent; moderately alkaline (pH 8.1); clear wavy boundary.
^Cu2--111 to 145 centimeters; variegated 50 percent brown (10YR 5/3) and 50 percent brownish yellow (10YR 6/6), clay loam, variegated 50 percent brown (10YR 4/3) and 50 percent dark grayish brown (10YR 4/2) moist; 10 percent dark gray (2.5Y 4/1) and 15 percent dark brown (7.5YR 3/4) mottles; massive, moderately hard, firm, moderately sticky, moderately plastic; 5 percent subrounded fine and medium gravel; 2 percent artifacts of paper; finely disseminated free carbonates in the soil matrix; very slightly effervescent; slightly alkaline (pH 7.6); abrupt smooth boundary.
2C--145 to 200 centimeters; brown (10YR 5/3), very gravelly coarse sand, dark yellowish brown (10YR 3/4) moist; single grain; loose, nonsticky, nonplastic; 55 percent subrounded fine and medium gravel; finely disseminated free carbonates throughout the matrix; very slightly effervescent; slightly alkaline (pH 7.8).
Type Location:
Denver County, CO
USGS Quadrangle: Arvada
Latitude: 39.7526639
Longitude: -105.0123028
Datum: WGS84
Coordinates sources: Hand-held GPS
Range in Characteristics:
Depth to free carbonates--10 to 25 centimeters
Thickness of human-transported material--50 to 200 centimeters
Particle-size control section (weighted average):
Clay content--18 to 35 percent
Sand content--30 to 75 percent
Total rock fragments--35 to 65 percent
^A or ^Au horizon(s):
Hue--7.5YR to 10YR
Value--3 to 5 dry; 1 to 3 moist
Chroma--2 to 5 dry; 2 to 4 moist
Texture (less than 2 mm fraction) -- Clay loam, sandy clay loam, loam
Clay content--12 to 35 percent
Rock fragments--0 to 30 percent gravel
Artifacts-0 to 15 percent
Calcium carbonate equivalent--0 to 3 percent
Reaction--pH 7.0 to 7.8
Thickness--5 to 20 centimeters
^C horizon(s) (when present):
Hue--7.5YR or 10YR
Value--4 to 6 dry; 3 to 5 moist
Chroma--3 to 5 dry or moist
Texture (less than 2 mm fraction) -- Clay loam, sandy clay loam, sandy loam
Clay content--15 to 35 percent
Rock fragments--10 to 50 percent gravel
Calcium carbonate equivalent--3 to 14 percent
Reaction--pH 7.4 to 8.0
Thickness--0 to 60 centimeters (combined)
^Cu horizon(s):
Hue-7.5YR or 10YR
Value--3 to 6 dry; 1 to 4 moist
Chroma--2 to 5 dry or moist
Texture (less than 2 mm fraction)--Clay loam, sandy clay loam, sandy loam
Clay content--10 to 35 percent
Rock fragments--5 to 60 percent
Artifacts--1 to 10 percent
Calcium carbonate equivalent--0 to 14 percent
Reaction--pH 7.4 to 8.2
Thickness--10 to 185 centimeters (combined)
2C horizon (s) (when present):
Hue--10YR
Value-4 or 5 dry; 3 or 4 moist
Chroma-3 or 4 dry or moist
Texture (less than 2 mm fraction)--Silty clay loam, sandy loam, coarse sand
Clay content--5 to 35 percent
Rock fragments--0 to 55 percent gravel
Calcium carbonate equivalent--3 to 5 percent
Reaction--pH 7.4 to 7.8
Thickness--0 to 125 centimeters (combined)
Competing Series:
There are currently no competing series.
Geographic Settings:
Parent material--Loamy-skeletal human-transported material over alluvium
Landform--Human-transported fill on stream terraces
Slope ranges--0 to 25 percent
Mean annual air temperature--10.0 to 12.0 degrees C (50.0 to 53.6 degrees F)
Mean annual precipitation range--356 to 432 millimeters (14.0 to 17.0 inches)
Frost-free period--135 to 165 days
Elevation--1,560 to 1,680 meters (~5,120 to 5,510 feet)
Climate--Cold and snowy winters; cool, moist springs, and hot summers with North American monsoon precipitation from July through August.
Geographically Associated Soils:
None
Drainage and Saturated Hydraulic Conductivity:
Drainage class: Well and moderately drained
Saturated hydraulic conductivity: Moderately high (1.5 to 5 m/sec)
Flooding: Altitude soils may be very rarely to rarely flooded from March through August
Ponding: Altitude soils may be rarely ponded from March through August
Use and Vegetation:
Most areas are primarily used for residential suburban development. The potential native vegetation is little bluestem, sideoats grama, winterfat, and skunkbush sumac.
Distribution and Extent:
Holocene stream terraces along the South Platte River in the Denver, Colorado area; small extent in north-central Colorado; MLRA 67B.
Soil Survey Region (SSR) Responsible:
Bozeman, Montana
Series Proposed:
Denver County, CO 2025. Altitude series is a coined name based on Denver's mile-high altitude.
Remarks:
Typical Pedon Taxonomic Features:
Ochric epipedon--0 to 14 centimeters (^A horizon)
Free carbonates-14 to 200 centimeters (^C, ^Cu1, ^Cu2, & 2C horizons)
Human-transported materials--0 to 145 centimeters (^A, ^C, ^Cu1, ^Cu2 horizons)
Artifacts--70 to 145 centimeters (^Cu1, ^Cu2 horizons)
Lithologic discontinuity--145 centimeters
Particle-size control section--25 to 100 centimeters
Taxonomic version: Keys to Soil Taxonomy, 13th edition, (2022)
